text.value: The reference http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medicinal-product-classification could not be resolved
The reference http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/unit-of-presentation could not be resolved
The reference http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/substance-codes could not be resolved
The reference integer64 could not be resolved
The reference CodeableReference could not be resolved
The reference RatioRange could not be resolved
The reference Availability could not be resolved
The reference ExtendedContactDetail could not be resolved
The IG is not for the international realm, and it uses SNOMED CT, so it should fix the SCT edition in the expansion parameters
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/unit-of-presentation' not found
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/unit-of-presentation' not found

The ImplementationGuide is based on FHIR version 4.0.1 but package hl7.fhir.eu.extensions#current is based on FHIR version 5.0.0. In general, this version mismatch should be avoided - some tools will try to make this work with variable degrees of success, but others will not even try

The ImplementationGuide is based on FHIR version 4.0.1 but package ihe.pharm.mpd#current is based on FHIR version 5.0.0. In general, this version mismatch should be avoided - some tools will try to make this work with variable degrees of success, but others will not even try
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medicinal-product-classification' not found
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medicinal-product-classification' not found
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/unit-of-presentation' not found

The extension http://profiles.ihe.net/PHARM/MPD/StructureDefinition/ihe-ext-medication-strengthsubstance is not allowed to be used at this point (allowed = e:Medication.ingredient.strength[x]; this element is [Medication.ingredient.strength, Ratio])

The extension http://profiles.ihe.net/PHARM/MPD/StructureDefinition/ihe-ext-medication-strengthsubstance is not allowed to be used at this point (allowed = e:Medication.ingredient.strength[x]; this element is [Medication.ingredient.strength, Ratio])
ValueSet 'http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/substance-codes' not found

This element does not match any known slice defined in the profile http://hl7.eu/fhir/mpd/StructureDefinition/MedicationRequest-eu-mpd|0.1.0 (this may not be a problem, but you should check that it's not intended to match a slice)
The extension http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/elementdefinition-maxValueSet|5.2.0 is deprecated
URL value 'integer64' does not resolve
URL value 'CodeableReference' does not resolve
URL value 'RatioRange' does not resolve
URL value 'Availability' does not resolve
URL value 'ExtendedContactDetail' does not resolve
Constraint failed: ext-ab-1: 'Additional Bindings SHOULD have a key to allow a binding to be constrained.'

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/ValueSet/medicine-active-substances-uv-ips'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). Using version '2.0.0', found versions: 1.1.0, 2.0.0. Suggested fix: change the canonical reference from 'http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/ValueSet/medicine-active-substances-uv-ips' to 'http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/ValueSet/medicine-active-substances-uv-ips|2.0.0'
Constraint failed: ext-ab-1: 'Additional Bindings SHOULD have a key to allow a binding to be constrained.'

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/ValueSet/medicine-active-substances-uv-ips'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). The string value contains text that looks like embedded HTML tags. If this content is rendered to HTML without appropriate post-processing, it may be a security risk
